extra.message: User input from the 'name' query parameter is interpolated directly into HTML via an f-string without any sanitization or escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (e.g., ?name=<script>alert(1)</script>) which will be reflected in the response.

User-controlled input (the 'name' query parameter) flows into content which is then rendered as a Jinja2 template via Template(content).render(). This enables Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) — an attacker can inject Jinja2 expressions (e.g., ?name={{config}}) to read server-side variables or achieve remote code execution.
Hardcoded secret name 'John Ripper' found in source code. The comment indicates this should be read from an environment variable (SUPER_SECRET_NAME) but it is hardcoded instead.

Hardcoded secret token '5u93R53Cr3Tt0k3n' found in source code. The comment indicates this should be read from an environment variable (SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN) but it is hardcoded instead. This token is exposed to anyone with access to the repository.
